# Feature: Scan orchestrator (--auto)

<!-- auto-generated by /implement on 2026-07-17 — do not edit manually unless correcting intent -->

## Intent
One command orchestrates: source → optional playwright import if present → sufficiency → on escalate: runtime (if enabled) + interactive crawl → re-evaluate → German next-steps if still blocked. Prefer explicit `--auto` / `scan.auto` to avoid surprising cost.

Roadmap: Autonomy batch C — depends on #144 + #145.

## Preconditions
- Sufficiency gate (#144) and interactive crawl (#145) on main
- Fixture HTTP server for crawl integration test

## Happy Path
- [ ] CLI `--auto` and config `scan.auto?: boolean`
- [ ] Does not require host JSON reporter
- [ ] Without import, `--auto`/`--crawl` yields ≥1 ordered flow OR clear escalate/blocked reasons
- [ ] FLOW_SEEDING_HINT primary story is autonomy crawl/auto; import optional

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Import present and sufficient → no forced crawl
- [ ] Escalate with no uncovered routes → crawl known routes or `/`
- [ ] Still blocked after auto → DE next-steps warning

## Regression
- [ ] Existing scan/import/doctor tests still pass
- [ ] Explicit `--crawl` without `--auto` still works

## Assumptions
- Runtime only when already enabled (options/config), not forced by `--auto`
- AI enrichment still out of scope for this batch

## Screenshots
| Step | Filename |
|------|----------|
| n/a | CLI only |

## Implementation Notes
- `scan.auto` config + CLI `--auto`
- Mid-pipeline sufficiency → crawl on escalate/blocked; runtime only if already enabled
- Empty uncovered → crawl known routes or `/`
- FLOW_SEEDING_HINT prioritizes `--auto`/`--crawl`; import optional
- Test: `scan-auto.integration.test.ts`
